behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive errors
behaviors.justification: Parsing a cron string to validate syntax is a deterministic algorithm with no I/O dependencies. Invalid inputs (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) trigger errors based purely on pattern matching and validation rules. Tests pass invalid strings directly and verify error messages. No mock can hide a validation bug because the parser either accepts or rejects deterministically.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test directly calls parser.parse() with invalid cron expressions ('13 * * * *' for invalid month, '32 * * * *' for invalid day, '* * * * * *' for too many fields) and verifies each raises appropriate exception with descriptive error message containing the violation (e.g., 'Month must be 0-12').

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps including last day of month and nearest weekday
behaviors.justification: Computing next run times is pure calendar arithmetic: given a cron schedule and start time, produce N future timestamps. Edge cases (last day of month, nearest weekday) are deterministic date math. Tests inject the start time as a parameter rather than relying on system clock. Algorithm correctness is testable without system state, database, or external time source.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test injects cron schedule ('0 9 L * *' for 9 AM on last day of month) and start time (Feb 15, 2025) and verifies next_run returns Feb 28 at 9 AM. Additional tests for nearest-weekday logic ('0 9 15W * *' on dates around the 15th) verify correct skipping to weekday when 15th is weekend. All inputs injectable; no system time involved.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector identifies conflicting schedules within minimum gap threshold
behaviors.justification: The overlap detector is a pure comparison algorithm: take two schedule objects and a gap threshold, return whether they conflict and report conflicting time windows. No external state, no mocking needed. Constructing two schedules and calling the detector is entirely in-process and deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test constructs two cron schedules ('*/10 * * * *' every 10 minutes and '*/15 * * * *' every 15 minutes) and threshold (5-minute gap), verifies detector correctly identifies minutes 0, 10, 20... (10-min) overlapping with 0, 15, 30... (15-min) as conflicts within the 5-min gap. Another test with non-overlapping schedules verifies no conflict reported.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling correctly handles DST transitions (spring-forward gaps and fall-back ambiguity)
behaviors.justification: DST handling is a deterministic algorithm: given a target time (2:30 AM) on a DST transition date in a specific timezone, determine if it exists and compute the correct run time. Uses Python's standard zoneinfo library (not mocked) which has correct DST rules. Tests inject start time, schedule, and timezone object; algorithm either correctly skips non-existent times or picks correct occurrence during ambiguous hour. Mocking zoneinfo would hide bugs in how code interprets DST transitions, so real tzdata is required. Still Unit because test is fully in-process with injected inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test injects start time (2025-03-09 01:50 EST, before spring forward) with schedule '30 2 * * *' (2:30 AM daily) and real ZoneInfo('America/New_York'), verifies next_run is 2025-03-09 03:30 EDT (skips non-existent 2:30 AM gap). Fall-back test: start time 2025-11-02 01:50 EDT with same schedule, verifies engine picks first occurrence of 2:30 AM (EDT) during the ambiguous overlap hour. Uses real system timezone database, not mocked.

behaviors.description: Missed-run policy engine correctly determines skip vs backfill for past run windows
behaviors.justification: The missed-run decision is a deterministic algorithm: given a last_run timestamp, current_time, and policy configuration, decide next_run (skip: next scheduled time, backfill: run immediately then reschedule). No persistent state, no system clock, no mocking needed. Tests inject all parameters directly and verify decision logic output.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test injects last_run (10:00 AM yesterday), current_time (10:15 AM today), policy='skip', and schedule (daily at 10:00 AM); verifies next_run is tomorrow at 10:00 AM (missed window skipped). Backfill test with same inputs but policy='backfill' verifies next_run is immediately at 10:15 AM (run now), then subsequent next_run is tomorrow at 10:00 AM. All times injectable; no system time accessed.
